mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving positive equity returns through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window where both INDPRO and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening regime (UNRATE < 4%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) creates inflationary pressures that force rate hikes, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where both UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing demand (HOUST) signals weak aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.10 for any 1-year window where both RSAFS and HOUST show negative momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates supply-side deterioration, creating headwinds for equity earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines below 105.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) reflect resilient consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved corporate revenue visibility.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 1-year window where HOUST shows positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1.5% signals aggressive monetary easing, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS declines below 1.5%.
